skill.name: post-show-followup
skill.description: Create post-trade-show follow-up email sequences, lead nurture campaigns, and meeting recap emails. Use this skill when the user needs to follow up with leads after a trade show, exhibition, expo, or conference — including tiered email sequences (hot/warm/cold), thank-you emails, meeting recap emails, badge-scan follow-ups, or any post-event outreach. Triggers on phrases like 'follow up after the show', 'post-show emails', 'write a thank you to people we met at [event]', 'we collected 200 leads at [show], help me write follow-up', 'the show just ended, now what', 'convert trade show leads into pipeline', 'trade show lead follow-up template', 'post-event email sequence', 'convert trade show leads', 'follow up with expo contacts', or 'I have a spreadsheet of contacts from the expo'. Also use this skill if the user mentions having just returned from a trade show and wants to do something with the contacts they collected.
skill.summary: Generate tiered follow-up email sequences that convert trade show conversations into sales pipeline within the critical 48-hour window.
skill.author: LensmorOfficial

security_audit.summary: All 43 static findings are false positives. This skill generates email templates as text output - it contains no executable code, scripts, network requests, or filesystem operations. The detected patterns (hardcoded URLs, backticks, path traversal sequences) are legitimate markdown documentation elements: marketing links, code block examples, and relative documentation links.

content.value_statement: Trade show leads often go unfollowed due to time constraints and lack of personalized outreach templates. This skill creates tiered email sequences based on lead warmth (hot/warm/cold) with personalized content that converts booth conversations into sales pipeline.
actual_capabilities: Generate 3-tier lead classification (hot/warm/cold) based on conversation quality
Create multi-email sequences with different angles per email for each tier
Insert personalization fields with bracket notation and CRM merge tags (HubSpot, Salesforce)
Provide recommended send timing to maximize open and reply rates
Adapt email tone and content based on deal complexity (transactional vs enterprise)
Include practical tips for post-show lead processing and CRM data entry
limitations: Does not actually send emails - only generates email content
Does not access or integrate with CRM systems directly
Does not provide lead scoring or prioritization algorithms
Requires user to provide event details, product info, and lead context

use_cases.title: Quick Post-Show Outreach
use_cases.description: Generate follow-up emails immediately after a trade show ends when leads are still fresh
use_cases.target_user: Sales teams returning from trade shows with badge scans

use_cases.title: Tiered Lead Nurture Campaign
use_cases.description: Create segmented email sequences based on conversation depth and expressed interest
use_cases.target_user: Marketing professionals building lead nurture workflows

use_cases.title: Meeting Recap Emails
use_cases.description: Generate personalized follow-up emails referencing specific conversations from the show
use_cases.target_user: Account executives who had demos or meetings at exhibitions

prompt_templates.title: Basic Post-Show Request
prompt_templates.prompt: [Show Name] just ended. We were showcasing [product/service]. Help me write follow-up emails for the leads we collected.

prompt_templates.title: Segmented Lead Follow-up
prompt_templates.prompt: We got [number] badge scans at [Show]. About [number] had real conversations, [number] asked for pricing. Write follow-up sequences for each group.

prompt_templates.title: Thank You Email for Demo
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a post-show thank you email for people who visited our demo at [Event]. We discussed [specific topics] with them.

prompt_templates.title: Large Lead Volume Processing
prompt_templates.prompt: I just got back from [Trade Show] with a spreadsheet of [number] contacts. Help me prioritize and write follow-ups. Our product is [description] and typical deal size is [range].
prompt_templates.scenario: High-volume lead processing with business context

output_examples.input: MEDICA just ended. We sell sterilization monitoring systems. Got about 150 badge scans, 40 real conversations, 12 asked for quotes.
output_examples.output:
A complete 3-tier email sequence with:

**Tier 1 - Hot (12 leads):** Day 1 email with quote, Day 4 value-add, Day 10 lower-commitment CTA
**Tier 2 - Warm (28 leads):** Day 2 connect + educate, Day 7 different angle
**Tier 3 - Cold (110 leads):** Day 5 soft intro + resource, Day 14 last touch

Includes subject lines, personalization fields, CRM merge tags, and recommended send timing.

output_examples.input: Write a thank you email for people who visited our booth at CES.
output_examples.output:
Professional thank you email template with:
- Personalization fields for recipient name and company
- Reference to the trade show experience
- Specific call-to-action (demo, call, resource)
- Signature with scheduling link option

best_practices: Send hot lead follow-ups within 24 hours while the conversation is fresh
Personalize emails with specific details from conversations, not generic templates
Use the appropriate CRM merge tags (HubSpot, Salesforce) for the user's system
anti_patterns: Sending the same generic 'great meeting you' email to all leads regardless of tier
Delaying follow-ups beyond 48 hours for hot leads - timing is critical
Including large file attachments instead of linking to resources

faq.question: What lead tiers does this skill create?
faq.answer: The skill creates 3 tiers: Hot (had real conversations, expressed clear interest), Warm (good conversation but exploratory), and Cold (badge scan only with no meaningful dialogue).

faq.question: Does this skill integrate with my CRM?
faq.answer: The skill generates email templates with the appropriate merge tags for popular CRMs (HubSpot, Salesforce, generic), but does not directly integrate or send emails.

faq.question: How many emails are in each sequence?
faq.answer: Hot leads get a 3-email sequence (Day 1, Day 4, Day 10), Warm leads get 2 emails (Day 2, Day 7), and Cold leads get 2 emails (Day 3-5, Day 14).

faq.question: Can I customize the timing recommendations?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill provides recommended timing but you can adjust based on your specific sales cycle and recipient time zones.

faq.question: What information should I provide for best results?
faq.answer: Provide the show name, what you were showcasing, any lead segmentation data you have, your typical deal size, and specific conversations you want to reference.

faq.question: Is this suitable for large lead volumes (100+)?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill includes guidance on prioritizing hot leads first and batching tier 2 and 3 processing efficiently.
